marketState: neutral-to-bearish — a strong near-term heat-driven demand spike is offset by a bearish medium-term backdrop (storage surplus, cooling 8-16D outlook, soft seasonality)
summary: Front-month NG ($2.936) is trading essentially at fair value ($2.917 fair, well inside the $2.42-$3.42 band), so there is little standalone pricing edge right now. Seasonality is soft: price is running 10.4pp below its typical seasonal path (YTD -13.8% vs -3.4% median) and the probability of a higher print falls to just 31% over 4 weeks, though a 50pp reliability spread this week means that read shouldn't be leaned on hard. Weather is the key near-term tension — 1-7 day demand is +23.5% above the 5-year norm near the top of its range (bullish for prompt burn), but the 8-16 day outlook flips to -6.3% below normal, meaning the heat catalyst likely fades right around the edge of a 1-10 day trade window. Medium-term fundamentals lean bearish: storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a +72 Bcf bearish surprise build, managed money is net short (though only at the 35th percentile, not a crowded/squeeze setup), and daily/weekly technicals (RSI 37-38, negative MACD, price below both EMAs) confirm a bearish trend underneath today's intraday bounce (5m RSI 68.8, price above the upper Bollinger band). The futures curve shows only mild front/next backwardation (-$0.042) alongside a large normal winter contango building into Dec/Jan ($3.78/$4.20), consistent with seasonal risk premium rather than acute near-term tightness.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk (trim size given Low seasonality reliability)
longTerm.rationale: Storage sits +184 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build, the 8-16D weather outlook cools to -6.3% vs 5y, and managed money is already net short with room to add (35th percentile). Seasonality's 31% odds of a higher 4-week print reinforce the bearish tilt, but the 50pp reliability spread and a fairly-priced front contract keep conviction low.
longTerm.invalidation: A shift to below-normal storage builds/deficits, an 8-16D weather outlook flipping back above 5y normal, or managed money flipping net long would invalidate this thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: The near-term drivers directly conflict: 1-7D heat demand is near the top of its 5y range (bullish, backed by mild front/next backwardation) while 4h-1w technicals show a confirmed bearish trend (negative MACD, price below both EMAs) and the 5m frame is already overbought (RSI 68.8, price above upper Bollinger). Front price is also sitting right at fair value, offering no pricing edge either way.
shortTerm.trigger: LONG if price pulls back to the $2.85-$2.90 fair-value support zone while 1-7D demand stays >15% above 5y; SHORT if price breaks below ~$2.85 with the 8-16D outlook confirming the heat rollover and daily trend still bearish.

keyDrivers: Storage +184 Bcf above 5y avg with a +72 Bcf bearish surprise build
1-7D weather demand +23.5% vs 5y (rangePos 0.92) vs 8-16D at -6.3% (rangePos 0.37) — heat catalyst fading
Daily/weekly technicals bearish (RSI 37-38, negative MACD, price below EMAs) despite an overbought 5m bounce
Seasonality skewed bearish (31% prob higher in 4wks) but reliability Low (50pp spread)
Managed money net short at 35th percentile — bearish tilt, not crowded
risks: Heat wave intensifies or extends beyond 16 days, keeping cooling demand elevated and squeezing the short thesis
Low seasonality reliability (50pp spread) means the seasonal edge could easily invert
A colder shift in the 8-16D outlook or a storage draw surprise would flip the medium-term bearish case
